title: Morning Robotics Scan
report:
## VT-WAM: Visual‑Tactile World Action Model

### The Breakthrough
VT‑WAM is a unified model that jointly predicts future visual frames, tactile deformations, and robot actions for contact‑rich manipulation. It introduces an Asymmetric Mixture‑of‑Transformers attention that links a static visual anchor to temporal tactile dynamics, plus a contact‑gated Action‑Visual‑Tactile Attention Guidance that forces actions to rely on tactile evidence. The system reaches a 71.67% average success rate across six real‑world tasks, substantially outperforming prior methods.

### The Caveats
The reported success is demonstrated only on six specific tasks and objects, with no evidence of generalization to unseen scenarios.

## QuadRocket: Adaptive Thrust‑Vector Testbed

### The Breakthrough
QuadRocket is a low‑cost quadrotor‑based platform that mimics a rocket with thrust‑vector actuation, enabling safe development of advanced control strategies. It models the vehicle as a single axisymmetric rigid body and uses a reduced‑attitude representation on the two‑sphere, coupled with an adaptive backstepping controller and a dynamic‑surface attitude controller that tracks thrust vectors without explicit differentiation. Experiments in an indoor motion‑capture arena show precise trajectory tracking and effective disturbance rejection.

### The Caveats
The paper provides no comparison to existing thrust‑vector control methods, leaving the relative performance unclear.

## Differentiable Quadrotor Interceptor

### The Breakthrough
The authors present an analytical policy‑gradient algorithm combined with differentiable quadrotor dynamics to train a controller that intercepts moving intruders using only a 3‑D direction vector from a monocular camera. Differentiable physics lets gradients flow through the flight dynamics, enabling end‑to‑end learning from direction‑only observations. In simulation the method achieves a 30% higher interception success rate than point‑mass baselines at speeds up to 10 m/s.

### The Caveats
The 30% gain is shown only in simulation against a simple baseline, with no real‑world quadrotor flights or camera validation.

## Embodied.cpp Portable Runtime

### The Breakthrough
Embodied.cpp is a C++ inference runtime that unifies deployment of vision‑language‑action and world‑action models across heterogeneous robot hardware via a five‑layer architecture (adapters, sequence builders, backbone, head plugins, deployment adapters). It enables latency‑first batch‑1 inference on edge devices, achieving 100% task success on HY‑VLA and 91% on pi0.5 while reducing WAM block memory from 312.2 MiB to 88.1 MiB.

### The Caveats
No comparison to existing inference runtimes is provided, so efficiency claims are unsubstantiated.

## AMD MI355X Cuts GLM‑5.2 Inference Cost

### The Breakthrough
Wafer demonstrates serving the GLM‑5.2 model on AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs at 2626 tokens/s per node (aggregate) and 213 tokens/s in single‑stream mode, yielding over 2× lower cost than comparable Nvidia Blackwell GPUs. The gain stems from 4‑bit MXFP4 quantization (AMD Quark) and a patched sglang stack with speculative decode, delivering a 3× speculative‑decode boost.

### The Caveats
Performance‑per‑dollar is measured only on a single‑node GLM‑1.5 benchmark, lacking multi‑node, cross‑model, or real‑world latency validation.

## Synaptic Harmony Swarm Foraging

### The Breakthrough
The repository offers a sandbox where dozens of agents learn to forage in a dynamic gridworld using independent NumPy Q‑tables, enabling fully distributed learning without external RL libraries. The environment features renewable resources, moving obstacles, and a YAML‑driven configuration with a live dashboard for rapid experimentation.

### The Caveats
No quantitative benchmark against existing multi‑agent RL methods is provided, so performance and scalability remain unvalidated.

## oomwoo DIY Robot Vacuum

### The Breakthrough
oomwoo is an open‑source robot vacuum built on a Raspberry Pi 5 (or ESP32 with micro‑ROS), ROS 2 Nav2, a low‑cost 2D LiDAR for SLAM, and Home Assistant integration, all packaged in a fully 3D‑printable chassis. The project supplies modular ROS 2 packages, firmware, and PCB designs to enable DIY construction matching commercial capabilities.

### The Caveats
No physical robot has been built or tested, so autonomous navigation and affordability claims are unproven beyond simulation.

## ReductStore Binary Time‑Series Store

### The Breakthrough
ReductStore is a Rust‑based storage engine that treats binary payloads (camera frames, ROS bags) as first‑class time‑series entries with arbitrary label filters, offering single‑node API, Docker deployment, replication, and lifecycle policies. The README cites >1 PB managed data, 60k+ downloads, and 100+ production deployments as early adoption evidence.

### The Caveats
The repository provides no quantitative performance or baseline comparisons, leaving superiority claims unverified.

### Priority
VT‑WAM delivers a concrete multimodal manipulation advance that directly raises real‑world task success, making it the most immediately relevant breakthrough for robotics researchers today.

### One action
Consider integrating VT‑WAM’s asymmetric MoT attention and tactile‑gated action guidance into your own manipulation pipelines.

### What to watch
A trend is emerging in aerial robotics: QuadRocket shows hardware‑centric thrust‑vector control on a physical testbed, while the Differentiable Quadrotor Interceptor pushes simulation‑first, gradient‑based policy learning using monocular direction cues. Watching how these approaches converge—hardware validation of differentiable dynamics—could shape next‑generation agile aerial systems.
